anti_claim: The first-screen composition root is a public compression contract only. It does not authorize release, hosted publication, provider calls, source mutation, private-data equivalence, score-based progress, or whole-system correctness.

anti_claim_contract.rule: Every first-screen composition surface must state that it routes attention and evidence order; it is not product maturity, release, or proof authority.

anti_claims: The first-screen composition root is a public compression contract only. It does not authorize release, hosted publication, provider calls, source mutation, private-data equivalence, score-based progress, or whole-system correctness.
A one-screen card is not a replacement for the full route map, receipts, standards, or workingness drilldowns.
Evidence counts on the card are accounting fields, not maturity scores.
Reader branches select what to inspect next; they do not create different authority ceilings for different audiences.
Reader exit criteria decide when to leave the first screen; they do not certify understanding, hiring readiness, or safety approval.
The discipline comparison strip explains inspectable differences; it is not a benchmark, superiority, or maturity claim.

Active v2 standard status is a source JSON contract state only; it does not expand release, publication, provider dispatch, private-root equivalence, product-readiness, or whole-system correctness authority beyond the authority_ceiling.

omission_receipt.reason: The first screen carries a bounded claim frame, reader branch handles, command refs, evidence-count context, omission handles, and authority ceilings.

omission_receipt_contract.rule: Composition roots must name what deeper drilldowns preserve instead of copying those bodies into the card.

receipt_expectations: receipt should cite the shared first command, reader branch ids, reader route menu, reader landing packets, behavior-proof packet, first-run ladder, first-viewport manifest, representative substrate glance, local state receipt trail, first-contact surface refs, overclaim tripwire matrix, discipline comparison strip, reader exit criteria, reader focus mode, evidence-accounting frame, comparison frame, doctrine-effect frame including concept and mechanism handles, README-entry contract, entry-surface contract, runnable-vs-structural join, observatory landing frame with a copyable localhost serve command, omission receipt, authority ceiling, and anti-claim when a runtime card consumes this standard

required_reentry: land each unresolved used_by_organs target as accepted_current_authority or remove/rename the target in this standard source
refresh aggregate doctrine-lattice projections only through the builder owner after source admission or source cleanup lands
non_laundering_rules: active v2 standard status does not accept unresolved used_by_organs targets
relationships.used_by_organs is a routing declaration, not runtime invocation or organ admission proof
standard registry draft status is inventory metadata, not evidence that unresolved targets are accepted
generated coverage, health, and entry-card residual rows are projections, not source evidence
used_by_organ_boundary.claim_ceiling: standard relationship residual only, not usage, acceptance, runtime, release, publication, provider dispatch, private-root equivalence, or whole-system correctness proof

source_coupling_contract.rule: Composition roots must cite public-root docs, standards, commands, and receipt refs only; dynamic counts must be dated or projected from owner receipts.

standard_payload.anti_purpose: A marketing hero, command list, or raw JSON dump that makes the reader infer what Microcosm is before seeing the evidence hierarchy.
failure_modes: first screen starts with a long command inventory instead of one shared behavior command
reader routes diverge before the shared local behavior card
reader route choices exist without copyable focused hello and text-card commands
reader branches name audiences without one first action, one proof surface, and one success criterion
the shared behavior command is named without success fields that tell a reader what local proof to inspect
the first screen names useful commands without a copyable run order from map to proof to status confirmation to reader branch
the first screen does not give terminal, README, browser, JSON, and video consumers the same ordered viewport slots
the first run writes local state but the first screen does not name the generated catalog, route, event, evidence, and graph refs
the first screen names generated state but does not compress route, work, event, evidence, graph, observatory, proof, and status handles into one cold-reader surface map
the first screen leaves readers to infer which common overclaims are invalid
the first screen says discipline is unusual without a side-by-side operational comparison
the first screen does not tell a reader when to stop reading and choose the next drilldown
the doctrine-effect frame omits concepts and mechanisms, leaving agents to search standards manually
the first screen exposes commands, counts, and boundaries but no representative examples of what the public organs actually do
honest evidence counts are shown without explaining that they are accounting fields rather than maturity scores
first-screen source-open material counts drift from the implemented-organ fixture manifests or workingness count source
breadth is listed as many topics without a composition root
runnable demo is shown without naming the larger structural surface it exercises
observatory endpoints are shown without the copyable local serve command that makes them reachable
doctrine constraints are shown without the failure modes they prevent
visual or observatory entry claims hosted publication or release authority
card omits the drilldown path that preserves the deeper route map, receipts, standards, and workingness evidence
standard_payload.ontology: A one-screen public compression contract that states what Microcosm is, the single shared first command, the copyable reader route menu, the copyable first-run ladder, the first-viewport manifest, a representative substrate glance from public atlas glosses, the local state receipt trail, the first-contact route/work/event/evidence/graph/observatory/proof/status surface refs, the overclaim tripwire matrix, the discipline comparison strip, the reader exit criteria, the behavior-proof success fields, the reader-specific first action/proof/success packet, the honest evidence-count frame, the runnable-to-structural join, the local observatory serve command with endpoint order, and the authority ceiling.
standard_payload.teleology: Let a cold reader understand the substrate before choosing a drilldown, without weakening the deeper route map, receipts, standards, or workingness surfaces.

contract_projection_basis.authority_boundary: computed standard-lattice contract only; existing authority_ceiling remains the operative claim ceiling.

validation_rules: require exactly one shared first command, `microcosm tour --card <project>`, before reader branching
require the shared first command to name behavior-proof fields: front_door_status.status, selected_route_id, state_inspection, and source_files_mutated
require a first-run ladder that orders hello, tour --card, status --card, and reader branch without making the long quickstart inventory first contact
require a first-viewport manifest that orders identity, first run, proof chain, evidence context, reader branch, and authority boundary across terminal, README, browser, JSON, and video consumers
require a local state receipt trail that names .microcosm catalog, routes, events, evidence, and graph refs without granting source, release, private-root, proof, benchmark, or maturity authority

require first-contact surface refs that compress route, work, events, evidence, graph, observatory, proof-lab, and status handles without exporting body text or granting source, release, provider, mutation, or proof authority
require an overclaim tripwire matrix that translates common cold-reader overclaims into valid bounded reads before drilldown
require reader exit criteria that tell each reader when the first screen is complete and which drilldown to open if it is not
require reader routes for public GitHub visitor, safety or evals engineer, hiring reviewer, peer developer, and Type A agent when reader branching is present
require a reader route menu with copyable focused `microcosm hello --reader <route> <project>` and `microcosm first-screen --format text --reader <route> <project>` commands for each route
require reader landing packets to carry one first action, one proof surface, one success criterion, one next drilldown, and no reader-specific authority ceiling
require evidence counts to be framed as accounting, not maturity, readiness, completeness, or progress scores

require a representative substrate glance derived from the public agent-task-route organ-glance projection so the first screen shows actual organ substance without becoming an inventory or readiness claim
require source-open material counts to derive from implemented-organ fixture manifests before falling back to the workingness receipt
require a comparison frame that names common first-entry failure modes and the Microcosm discipline that prevents them
require a discipline comparison strip that contrasts ordinary cold-entry failure patterns with Microcosm's visible boundaries without making benchmark or superiority claims
require a doctrine-effect frame that presents doctrine handles as prevented mistakes with visible first-screen effects
require a README-entry contract that orders the package-backed first-screen card before install, direct-run, and full quickstart command inventories
require an entry-surface contract that lets CLI, README, and observatory consumers reuse the package-backed card without changing its authority ceiling
require a runnable-vs-structural join that names both local behavior and larger substrate scale
require an observatory landing frame that pairs localhost endpoint refs with the copyable `microcosm serve <project> --host 127.0.0.1 --port 8765` command
require terminal text projections to fit in one screen and preserve the same authority ceiling as the JSON contract
require focused reader text projections to preserve the shared first command, evidence-count frame, omission receipt, and authority ceiling
require an omission receipt pointing to deeper route maps, receipts, standards, and workingness drilldowns
reject release, hosting, publication, provider-call, private-equivalence, score-progress, and whole-system-correctness overclaims
require public-root relative refs or explicit localhost endpoint refs

author.purpose: create a valid First-screen composition root instance

refine_instance.purpose: improve an existing First-screen composition root without corrupting authority

refine_standard_and_propagate.purpose: change std_microcosm_first_screen_composition_root and propagate through governed artifacts plus generated lattice projections

source_authority_ref: microcosm-substrate/AXIOMS.md#1 (JSON is contract; Markdown is projection)

doctrine_kind_envelope.note: This standard conforms to the universal doctrine-kind envelope; markdown, Mermaid, Atlas, coverage, health, and entry-card views are generated projections of this JSON source.

runtime_acceptance_refs.uplift_basis: registry-backed source standard already carried accepted, active, source-body-import, or body-floor status; this update narrows it into the active v2 JSON contract shape without raising its authority ceiling.
